steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $69$, so $m=69$. The value $f(0)=3$ means the $y$-intercept is $3$, so $b=3$. Therefore $$f(x)=69x+3.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A has intercept $0$, choice B has slope $3$, and choice C has intercept $0$. Only choice D has slope $69$ and $f(0)=3$. Choice D is correct.
